---
title: "4654LargeDataset"
author: "Arnav Gupta"
date: "2026-04-23"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

```{r}
# -------------------------------
# 1. LOAD DATA
# -------------------------------

# Load dataset
df <- read.csv("anes_timeseries_cdf_csv_20260205.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


# -------------------------------
# 2. KEEP ONLY RELEVANT VARIABLES
# -------------------------------

vars <- c("VCF0702",  # turnout
          "VCF0101",  # age
          "VCF0110",  # education
          "VCF0114",  # income
          "VCF0301",  # party ID
          "VCF0310",  # political interest
          "VCF0009z") # weights

df_clean <- df[, vars]


# -------------------------------
# 3. CLEAN DATA
# -------------------------------

# Replace ANES missing values (negative numbers) with NA
df_clean[df_clean < 0] <- NA


# -------------------------------
# 4. RECODE VARIABLES (BASED ON CODEBOOK)
# -------------------------------

# Dependent Variable: Turnout (1 = voted, 0 = did not vote)
df_clean$vote <- ifelse(df_clean$VCF0702 == 2, 1,
                  ifelse(df_clean$VCF0702 == 1, 0, NA))

# Age
df_clean$age <- as.numeric(df_clean$VCF0101)

# Education (baseline = highest education)
df_clean$education <- factor(df_clean$VCF0110,
                            levels = c(4,3,2,1),
                            labels = c("CollegePlus", "SomeCollege", "HighSchool", "LessHS"))

# Income (higher = richer)
df_clean$income <- as.numeric(df_clean$VCF0114)

# Party ID (baseline = Independent)
df_clean$party_id <- factor(df_clean$VCF0301,
                           levels = c(4,3,5,2,6,1,7),
                           labels = c("Independent",
                                      "IndLeanDem",
                                      "IndLeanRep",
                                      "WeakDem",
                                      "WeakRep",
                                      "StrongDem",
                                      "StrongRep"))

# Political Interest (higher = more interested)
df_clean$interest <- as.numeric(df_clean$VCF0310)

# Keep weights
df_clean$weight <- df_clean$VCF0009z


# Drop missing values AFTER recoding
df_clean <- na.omit(df_clean)


# -------------------------------
# 5. PEARSON CORRELATIONS
# -------------------------------

#ivs <- c("age", "income", "interest")

#for (var in ivs) {
#  cat("\n--------------------------------------\n")
#  cat("Correlation between vote and", var, ":\n")
#  print(cor.test(df_clean$vote, df_clean[[var]], method = "pearson"))
#}


# -------------------------------
# 6. PROBIT REGRESSION (MODEL 1)
# -------------------------------

model1 <- glm(vote ~ age + education + income + party_id + interest,
              data = df_clean,
              weights = weight,
              family = binomial(link = "probit"))

summary(model1)


# -------------------------------
# 7. MARGINAL EFFECTS
# -------------------------------

# Install if needed
if (!require(margins)) install.packages("margins")

library(margins)

mfx <- margins(model1)
summary(mfx)
```

```{r}
write.csv(df_clean, 
          "anes_cleaned.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```
